skill.name: datamol
skill.description: Datamol guidance for using Python and RDKit to parse, standardize, analyze, visualize, and process molecular datasets.
skill.summary: Use Datamol and RDKit for practical cheminformatics workflows.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis produced many high-risk hits, but review found they are primarily Markdown backticks, chemistry terminology, and documented Datamol examples. No prompt injection, malicious network use, credential collection, or hidden code execution evidence was found. Publishable with low-risk disclosure for package installation, file I/O, and optional remote storage access.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are primarily Markdown inline code and fenced Python examples, not Ruby shell backtick execution. The only shell command found is a normal package installation example for datamol.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations contain Markdown code spans or fenced examples. They document Datamol API calls and do not invoke a shell except for the explicit install command.

low_findings.title: Chemistry Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match chemistry vocabulary such as SMILES, InChI keys, fingerprints, and molecular descriptors. No cryptographic implementation or security-sensitive hashing was found in the reviewed content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms appear in a cheminformatics context and describe molecular representations or features. There is no evidence of password hashing, encryption, or certificate handling.

low_findings.title: Documented Remote File Access Requires User Control
low_findings.description: The skill explains Datamol remote I/O through fsspec for S3, GCS, Azure, and HTTP paths. This is a legitimate data feature, but users should avoid sending sensitive molecular datasets to untrusted URLs or buckets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicitly documented as remote file support. The examples use placeholder locations and do not show credential exfiltration or hidden endpoints.

low_findings.title: Local File Read and Write Guidance
low_findings.description: The skill includes examples for reading and writing SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON, MOL, and PDB files. This is expected for molecular data workflows, but it grants broad file handling capability when followed by an assistant.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file operations are standard Datamol API examples. They are not malicious, but they are real filesystem capabilities that should be disclosed.

content.value_statement: Cheminformatics tasks often require many RDKit calls and careful molecular data handling. This skill provides Datamol workflows for parsing, cleaning, analyzing, visualizing, and processing molecules.
actual_capabilities: Guides Datamol installation, import conventions, and basic molecule creation from SMILES, InChI, SELFIES, and SMARTS.
Shows how to standardize, sanitize, fix, and convert molecules while keeping RDKit molecule compatibility.
Documents reading and writing SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON, MOL, and PDB molecular data files.
Supports descriptor, fingerprint, similarity, clustering, and diversity-selection workflows for compound libraries.
Covers 3D conformer generation, conformer analysis, molecular visualization, fragments, scaffolds, reactions, and toy datasets.
Includes practical guidance for batch processing, parallel jobs, cloud paths, and machine learning feature generation.
limitations: Requires Datamol, RDKit, and related scientific Python packages in the user environment.
Advanced RDKit customization may require direct RDKit use beyond the simplified Datamol interface.
Full distance clustering can become memory intensive for large libraries above roughly one thousand structures.
Remote file access depends on fsspec support, provider libraries, permissions, and user-controlled storage paths.

use_cases.title: Clean Compound Libraries
use_cases.description: Standardize SMILES, remove invalid molecules, compute descriptors, and export clean datasets for downstream screening.

use_cases.title: Build Molecular ML Features
use_cases.description: Generate fingerprints, descriptor tables, scaffold splits, and training features compatible with common Python ML workflows.

use_cases.title: Explore Structure Activity Relationships
use_cases.description: Group molecules by scaffold, compare similarity, visualize aligned series, and inspect fragment or reaction patterns.

prompt_templates.title: Parse and Clean SMILES
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Datamol to parse this list of SMILES, standardize valid molecules, report failures, and return clean canonical SMILES.

prompt_templates.title: Compute Descriptors
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Datamol workflow that reads my compound table, computes common molecular descriptors, and summarizes drug-likeness filters.

prompt_templates.title: Select Diverse Compounds
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Datamol fingerprints and diversity selection to choose a representative subset from my molecule library for screening.

prompt_templates.title: Design SAR Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Datamol workflow for scaffold grouping, descriptor calculation, aligned visualization, and SAR summary using my activity column.

output_examples.input: A CSV file with a SMILES column and inconsistent molecule formatting.
output_examples.output: A cleaned compound table, a list of invalid rows, standardized SMILES, and descriptor summaries for each valid molecule.

output_examples.input: A library of candidate molecules and a target number of compounds to screen.
output_examples.output: A ranked diverse subset with similarity rationale, representative scaffolds, and suggested visual checks.

output_examples.input: A scaffold series with activity values for each compound.
output_examples.output: A scaffold-based SAR summary with activity ranges, aligned molecule views, and notable descriptor trends.

best_practices: Standardize and sanitize molecules before descriptor, fingerprint, clustering, or visualization workflows.
Check for failed molecule parsing and keep invalid rows available for review.
Use parallel processing carefully and prefer diversity selection when full clustering becomes too large.
anti_patterns: Do not run large full-distance clustering jobs without estimating memory use first.
Do not send proprietary molecular datasets to remote URLs or buckets without confirming permissions.
Do not assume Datamol defaults replace direct RDKit control for advanced or unusual chemistry requirements.

faq.question: What is Datamol used for?
faq.answer: Datamol simplifies common RDKit cheminformatics workflows for molecule parsing, standardization, descriptors, fingerprints, visualization, and file I/O.

faq.question: Does this skill replace RDKit?
faq.answer: No. It guides Datamol usage, which returns native RDKit molecule objects and remains compatible with direct RDKit workflows.

faq.question: Can it process molecular files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill covers SDF, SMILES, CSV, Excel, Parquet, JSON, MOL, PDB, and remote paths supported by fsspec.

faq.question: Can it help with machine learning datasets?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes fingerprints, descriptor tables, scaffold splitting, diversity selection, and examples for feature generation.

faq.question: What are the main performance limits?
faq.answer: Conformer generation and full distance clustering can be slow or memory intensive for large molecule libraries.

faq.question: Is remote storage access automatic?
faq.answer: No. Remote access requires user-supplied paths, credentials, installed provider libraries, and compatible fsspec configuration.
